# Dagonizer

> Type-safe DAG dispatcher for Node.js. JSON-Schema-validated graph definitions, abortable execution, deterministic resume, pluggable gather strategies, contract-derived flow generation, and Mermaid visualization.

Dagonizer (`@noocodex/dagonizer`) is a TypeScript library for authoring and executing directed acyclic graphs (DAGs) as typed, abortable workflows. Nodes implement a narrow `NodeInterface<TState, TOutput>`; the dispatcher routes each node's named output to the next placement, handling scatter (fork over a source array), embedded-DAG composition (cardinality-1 nested DAG invocation), retry policies, timeouts, and checkpoint/resume without consumer-visible callbacks or hidden state. The wire format is JSON-LD 1.1; every DAG document is a linked-data artifact that validates against a published JSON Schema 2020-12. Zero runtime dependencies beyond `ajv` and `json-schema-to-ts`.

Dagonizer is **not** a visual drag-and-drop tool, a serverless orchestration service, or a general-purpose task queue. It is a typed, embeddable graph runtime for Node.js that consumers extend by subclassing, not by patching.

Key features: strict TypeScript (exactOptionalPropertyTypes), JSON-LD canonical wire format, embeddedDAG sub-graph composition, AbortSignal-propagating cancellation, per-node timeout with NodeTimeoutError, pluggable retry/backoff, Ajv-validated schema loading, checkpoint and deterministic resume, contract-derived flow generation, Mermaid and Cytoscape visualization.

## Getting started

- [Home](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/): project overview, feature summary, and quick-start.
- [Getting Started](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/getting-started): install, the smallest possible DAG, running the dispatcher.
- [Architecture](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/architecture): how the engine routes nodes, manages state, and composes embedded-DAGs.
- [Concepts](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/concepts): core mental model covering nodes, placements, outputs, scatter, embedded-DAG, and state.

## Live demo

- [The Archivist](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/examples/the-archivist): runnable in-browser biblio-assistant demo. Multi-source scatter, RDF memory graph, checkpoint/resume, PROV-O activity log, and DAG visualization, all driven by the live engine.

## Usage guide

- [Subclassing State](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/guide/subclassing): extend NodeStateBase for domain-specific state with checkpointable fields.
- [DAGBuilder](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/guide/builder): fluent builder API for constructing DAG documents programmatically.
- [Schema & JSON loading](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/guide/schema): load DAG definitions from external JSON or JSON-LD files via Validator.
- [Cancellation](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/guide/cancellation): AbortSignal propagation; cancel a running flow at any depth.
- [Retry](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/guide/retry): per-node retry policies, backoff strategies, and timeout integration.
- [Services](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/guide/services): dependency injection pattern for wiring external services into nodes.
- [Observability](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/guide/observability): lifecycle hooks (onFlowStart, onFlowEnd, onNodeStart, onNodeEnd, onError).
- [State accessors](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/guide/state-accessor): pluggable path-resolution for reading typed state across embedded-DAG boundaries.
- [Checkpoint](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/guide/checkpoint): snapshot typed state mid-flow; resume from a saved cursor.
- [Persistence](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/guide/persistence): store and reload checkpoint data across process restarts.
- [Contract-derived flows](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/guide/derive): generate a DAG topology automatically from a typed contract interface.
- [Visualization](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/guide/visualization): render any DAG (including embedded-DAG expansions) as Mermaid or Cytoscape.

## Examples

- [Phase 01 · Linear intake](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/examples/01-linear): simplest sequential node chain; the entry point for every Dagonizer learner.
- [Phase 02 · DAGBuilder](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/examples/02-builder): build a DAG document at runtime with the fluent builder rather than JSON.
- [Phase 03 · Tool schemas](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/examples/03-schema): JSON Schema 2020-12 tool input schemas in the Archivist; shape-only examples and CanonicalId deduplication.
- [Phase 04 · Scatter scout](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/examples/04-scatter): parallel scouts in a ScatterNode; collect results with gather strategies.
- [Phase 05 · Embedded-DAG composition](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/examples/05-embedded-dags): compose reusable embedded-DAGs inside a parent DAG without copying topology.
- [Phase 06 · Cancellation](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/examples/06-cancellation): abort a running flow; observe which nodes see the signal.
- [Phase 07 · Retry](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/examples/07-retry): wrap a node in a retry loop; configure exponential backoff and max attempts.
- [Phase 08 · Checkpoint + resume](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/examples/08-checkpoint): snapshot mid-flow, serialize state, and resume deterministically from the cursor.

## Reference

- [Dagonizer](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/reference/dagonizer): the main dispatcher class, covering execute, hooks, and extension surface.
- [Execution](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/reference/execution): ExecutionResult, ExecuteOptions, and the execute() call contract.
- [Nodes](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/reference/nodes): placement types (SingleNode, ScatterNode, EmbeddedDAGNode, TerminalNode, PhaseNode).
- [Lifecycle](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/reference/lifecycle): DAGLifecycleMachine states and transition guards.
- [Runtime](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/reference/runtime): Clock, Scheduler, RetryPolicy, RealTimeScheduler, BackoffStrategy.
- [Contracts](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/reference/contracts): NodeInterface, ClockProvider, SchedulerProvider, SchedulerHandle.
- [Core](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/reference/core): GatherStrategy, GatherStrategies, OutcomeReducers.
- [Derive](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/reference/derive): contract-to-DAG derivation API.
- [Viz](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/reference/viz): CytoscapeRenderer and MermaidRenderer APIs.
- [Validation](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/reference/validation): Validator and EntityValidator; Ajv compiled once at module load.
- [Checkpoint](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/reference/checkpoint): Checkpoint class, StateRestoreFnType, cursor serialization format.
- [Entities](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/reference/entities): JSON Schema 2020-12 definitions and FromSchema-derived TypeScript types.
- [Testing](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/reference/testing): VirtualClockProvider and VirtualScheduler for deterministic unit tests.
- [Errors](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/reference/errors): DAGError hierarchy (NodeTimeoutError, validation errors, lifecycle errors).

## Optional

- [CHANGELOG](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/feed.xml): RSS feed of per-version changelog entries.
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/Studnicky/Dagonizer): source, issues, and pull requests.
- [npm package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@noocodex/dagonizer): `@noocodex/dagonizer`; install, version history, and subpath exports.
- [Sitemap](https://studnicky.github.io/Dagonizer/sitemap.xml): machine-readable page index.
